title: Codex Hooks Config Schema
description: Schema for the generated plugins/<name>/hooks/codex-hooks.json — referenced by plugins/<name>/.codex-plugin/plugin.json's 'hooks' field (R20).
$comment: Repo-derived schema, not an official OpenAI schema. docs/research/2026-07-16-codex-plugin-contract-spike.md finding (d) confirms the manifest's 'hooks' string-path pointer is accepted by the parser, but hook execution itself is currently inert (plugin_hooks feature 'removed' on codex-cli 0.144.1), so this file's internal shape has never been exercised by a live Codex session. The top-level 'hooks' wrapper and the 'statusMessage' field are confirmed against a live fetch of learn.chatgpt.com/docs/build-plugins on 2026-07-19 (the documented default hooks/hooks.json example: {"hooks": {"SessionStart": [{"hooks": [{"type": "command", "command": ..., "statusMessage": ...}]}]}}). That example omits 'matcher' entirely and the doc text doesn't say whether it's required or even valid, so this schema keeps 'matcher' as this repo's pre-existing optional field (mirroring schemas/plugin.schema.json's inlineHooks) rather than dropping it on unconfirmed grounds. 'timeout' is likewise kept as optional (ported from the Claude hooks shape, not contradicted by the doc's non-exhaustive field list) alongside the newly-confirmed 'statusMessage'. 'commandWindows' (added for the gt-workflow Codex pilot, shell 04) is the documented Windows command override key alongside 'command' (docs/solutions/integration-issues/codex-plugin-manifest-and-hook-contract.md); emit-codex.js's buildCodexHookConfig always sets it to the same value as 'command' since Node entrypoints are platform-uniform. Re-verify and correct when upstream restores plugin_hooks and a live round-trip becomes possible.
